g.text: Why can’t even very high-quality SFT data replace preference data?

text.tspan: During training, the model is mainly encouraged to imitate the given target tokens, without

g.text: knowing the relative quality of other potential answers. For complex questions with multiple reasonable but uneven-
quality answers, SFT struggles to teach the model to choose responses that are more aligned with human intuition,

text.tspan: By introducing Chosen / Rejected comparative signals, preference data suppresses

g.text: response regions that are grammatically correct but inconsistent with desired values or logic.
• Train a reward model (RM) to support RLHF
Online Feedback and Continuous Optimization Pipeline
